column1: # Gold manifest for the phi-scan deterministic-scanner eval.
# Columns (tab-separated): fixture  role  class  expected_count  note
# Derived by RUNNING phi-scan/scripts/phi_check.py over each fixture from a
# neutral temp dir with --include-test-data (see recall-precision.sh and
# LEDGER.md for why). Counts are OBSERVED behavior, not guesses.
# role:  positive      = planted detectable PHI; counts toward the RECALL hard gate.
#        negative      = PHI-adjacent noise; any finding is a FALSE POSITIVE (informational only).
#        expected-miss = names/MRNs the deterministic layer cannot detect (SKILL.md); NOT gated.
# class: exact identifier label the scanner prints. "zip_5(restricted)" is the
#        HIPAA restricted-prefix variant and is tracked separately from "zip_5".
# class "_none_" with count 0 declares a fixture that must produce zero findings.

column5: @example.com/.org; scanner marks [TEST] but still DETECTS (recall unaffected)
varied formats incl parenthesized, +1, dotted, bare 10-digit
FALSE POSITIVE: version string 1.2.3.4 parsed as IPv4
FALSE POSITIVE: bare 5-digit numbers (build/invoice/qty/threshold x2) parsed as ZIP
names & MRNs not regex-detectable; deterministic layer correctly yields nothing (triage-only)
